feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Two applications for SAP Build Work Zone Standard in IAS
entry.content:
Dear Gurus

I have published SAP Build Work Zone standart in BTP trial subaccount.

Now in SAP Cloud Identity service I can see two applications.

What is the purpose of second application?

Which application I need to select from user provisioning?

Thank you

Regards

Vladimir Kogan

entry.title: Fetch user details (country) from IAS in the approuter
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I am working on a Cloud Foundry Approuter in SAP BTP and have the following custom middleware code:

 

const approuter = require('@sap/approuter');

const ar = approuter();

ar.beforeRequestHandler.use('/', function (req, res, next) {
    if (req.url.includes('/sap/opu/odata/MY_SERVICE/')) {
        req.headers.email_user = req.user.name;
        console.log("username");
        console.log(req.user.name);
        console.log("headers");
        console.log(req.headers);
        console.log("req");
        console.log(req);
    }
    
    next();
});

ar.start();

 

 

In this setup, I am trying to retrieve the country of the authenticated user. I know the user's email is accessible via req.user.name and I have also found the JWT Access Token in the request. However, the token does not include any region or country information.

Since country information is maintained in SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service), I’m wondering:

  • Is it possible to retrieve the country of the user from the req object or its associated JWT token?
  • If not, what would be the recommended approach to fetch country information from IAS in an SAP BTP App Router application?

Any pointers or advice would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!
Mohammed

entry.title: Managing User Roles Directly in Azure
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
We use Azure as an IdP, SAP Cloud Identity Services as a proxy, and various cloud apps. Is there a way to grant access to users who are accessing a cloud app directly in Azure, i.e., to assign roles (what a user can do within the cloud app)? In other words, to assign roles to the users in Azure and not in the cloud app? And is this even recommended?

Thanks you very much.

 

Best regards

entry.title: Self-registration workflow in IAS
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

is there a way to set up a self-registration workflow in SAP IAS where users can submit a request for account creation, when Azure is used as IdP ?

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: SuccessFactors Learning Access: "error: unable to retrieve idp credential@sf_idp_metadata"
entry.content:
Hello, 

has anyone ever seen the following error message:
"error: unable to retrieve idp credential@sf_idp_metadata"

It appears when a specific user wants to access our SuccessFactors Learning application. The user logs in to SuccessFactors Platform successfully (through SSO), but then fails to connect to SF Learning module (integrated Learning). What and where could be the cause for that issue?

Thanks a lot in advance,
Nina

entry.title: IPS Trasformation add Org Member Roles
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to read users from the SAP IAS and automatically assign Org Members via IPS SAP BTP Type Platform Members (Cloud Foundry).
I have made the configuration.
https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/sap-btp-platform-members-cloud-foundry-f5cefa9509d64b0a8aeab24a895feec5
With the default transformation I get the following error
group:b499810c-3102-4a73-84ae-7aa78c97e542,
system=MyTargetSystem,
time=2025-01-28T12:31:00.382+00,
error=Unable to write entity to target system with name rd-tgt-btp-rs-u-cloudfoundry-Administrator Caused by: An unparsed group id must have exactly 2 or 3 logical parts,
content={“displayName”:“Org Manager”,“members”:[{“$ref”:“https://myhost.accounts.ondemand.com/scim/Users/50c58b3b-347c-4ae0-8c7f-86a9b50780c2”,“type”:“User”,“value”:“50c58b3b-347c-4ae0-8c7f-86a9b50780c2”}],“urn:sap☁️scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:Group”:{“name”:“org_manager”}}
I think it is missing
//the organization group extension name follows the pattern: <org_ID> <org_role>
But I don't understand how to insert this into the transformation.
Greetings Dirk

entry.title: Cloud identity service SCIM api to retrieve personal information
entry.content:
Using the SCIM api, for example  https://xxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com/scim/Users I'm able to get informations about users and group of the IAS through basic authorization for my admin account, and through  bearer token for a system user. But, if I want to get my personal information, and I'm not an Admin user, but easily a user created into IAS, how can i get my informations? 

There is a particular endpoint to use for get my details?

If I try with the current SCIM andpoint I receive 403.

entry.title: How to access SAP Cloud Foundry Platform users SCIM API using Postman?
entry.content:
Dear Gurus.

Is it possible to access Cloud Foundry SCIM API using Postman? 

The same users that are managed as described here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/sap-btp-platform-members-cloud-foundry-f5cefa9509d64b0a8aeab24a895feec5

Thank you.

Regards

Vladimir

entry.title: SSO Multiple Emarsys accounts against single SAP IAS Tenant
entry.content:
Hi experts.

We are in a situation where we need some help configuring SSO for two Emarsys accounts, against a single IAS Tenant (our production IAS Tenant).

We have two accounts, say EMARSYS-01 and EMARSYS-02

We manually created the application for EMARSYS-01 in our IAS tenant, following this tutorial Single Sign-On:: How to enable Single Sign-On with SAP Identity Authentication Service – Emarsys

The first acoount worked like a charm at first try (from IAS side and from Emarsys side), so we went for the second account, EMARSYS-02.

Again, following the previous oficial tutorial we found that we can't duplicate the entity ID, for this tenant.

So we tried to put something like "suite-sso-emarsys-02"  in the "name" field while creating the second application.

When we tried to configure SSO, it give us the common error "Error: Identity Provider could not process the authentication request received. Delete your browser cache and stored cookies, and restart your browser. If you still experience issues after doing this, please contact your administrator."

Fiddling in the communication, we've found that Emarsys is sending "suite-sso" as issuer. That's is the reason for the previous error.

<ns1:Issuer Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:entity">suite-sso </ns1:Issuer>

We can't find where to change this ID on Emarsys, and we need to have both accounts on SAP IAS (or even more if we keep working with the solution).

entry.title: Creation custom attribute cloud identity service not visible
entry.content: HI! I am trying to create a custom attribute in my cloud identity service, and then see in the login details and use it in the SCIM api request.  However, I am not getting positive results. Even after following the guide 'https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-user-attributes-sent-to-application,' once the custom field is created, it is not visible among the attributes of old and new users.  How can I do this? thanks

entry.title: Subject Name Identifiier in IAS
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a question regarding the Subject Name Identifier in IAS.

I’ve been experimenting in my test environment and here's what I did:

  • In Azure, I created a Claim employee_id=user.employee_id.
  • In IAS, I set the Subject Name Identifier to Corporate Identity Provider=employee_id.
  • The user was created in SAC/BTP with the User Name being the employee_id (User Name filed = 131414)

Then I changed the employee_id in Azure, but the user was still able to log in.

Only when I removed the email=user.email claim in Azure (ODIC), the user could no longer log in to BTP.

I thought the SNI was the main factor for identifying the user in the cloud app.

So, for example, if the user with SNI=234324 is sent to BTP, and only the user with SNI (User Name in Cloud App) 1111 exists in BTP, the user should no longer be able to log in – but this is not the case. It seems that, even though the setting in IAS - Subject Name Identifier=employee_id , the user is identified/found by the email address in BTP

Why is that? Or did I misunderstand the purpose of the Subject Name Identifier?

Do I understand it correctly that the Subject Name Identifier is always equal to the User Name in a cloud app (SNI = User Name in SAC)

Thank you very much.

Best Regards

entry.title: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content:
I have a customer who is currently using basic authentication (ID and passwords). It seems like we have 2 articles regarding basic authentication’s depreciation timeline.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/55e0c3af883748749c308f3b719a4c01.html

And

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/1083c36fc3c34515a37266ce52811f7e.html

 

Which is the correct Emd of maintenance date? June or November?

If the customer experience some difficulties regarding their login after June, will SAP product team take care?

entry.title: Disable user in ABAP after deletion in EntraID
entry.content:
For audit reasons, you do not want to delete users in the ABAP system when a person leaves the organization. So it is desirable to lock that useraccount instead of a deletion. 

I am reading the users from EntraID to IAS and push them to S/4. If a user is now deleted in EntraID, that user gets deleted in S/4. I could set the parameter to not delete the user in ABAP, but than I will still be active, which is an issue with the license count in S/4

 

So, is there a way to lock the user in S/4 when it gets deleted from EntraID/IAS?

 

thanks

Sander

entry.title: IPS Transformation - Condition not working
entry.content:
Hello guys,

We are mapping attributes with IPS transformations (Source Entra ID / Target IAS) and I am having an issue when setting a condition that is not working. (line 18)

 

            {
                "sourcePath": "$.givenName",
                "targetPath": "$.userName",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "substring",
                        "beginIndex": 0,
                        "endIndex": 1
                    },
                    {
                        "function": "concatString",
                        "suffix": "$.surname"
                    },
                    {
                        "condition": "($.surname.length() > 11)",
                        "function": "substring",
                        "beginIndex": 0,
                        "endIndex": 12
                    }
                ],
                "correlationAttribute": true
            },

 

Whitout the condition "condition": "($.surname.length() > 11)" it is working fine but when there is a login name which is less than 12 characters I get an error during the provisioning job.

So I would need the last part (lines 17 to 22) to run only when a login name is longer than 12 characeters but their must be a syntax problem on my condition.

Any ideas?

Cheers

N

CC @yogananda @Colt @MartinRaepple

entry.title: IPS Delta Read
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have defined the following properties in the Source System (Azure): ips.full.read.force.count=3.

In the Target System (Identity Authentication), I have defined this property: ips.delete.existedbefore.entities=true.

Then, I deleted a group in IAS. I thought that when the READ job is executed 3 times (which would be Delta Read), a Full Read would be triggered on the 4th run, and the deleted group would be re-provisioned from the Source System to the Target System.

However, this is not happening.

What am I doing wrong?

How can I schedule the Delta Read job? I cannot find the ips.delta.read=enebaled parameter in either the Source or the Target system.

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: OpenID for SAC
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is it possible to configure OpenID Connect for user authentication  (SSO)  between SAC and IAS, when IAS is configured as a proxy ?

Or is SAML 2.0 the only supported and recommended method?

Thank you.

entry.title: SSO Error in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help.

I have configured IAS as a proxy, and in IAS, I have set the Subject Name Identifier=employee_id

In Azure (OpenID Connection to IAS), I have added the employee_id to Additional Claims.

In the source system (Azure) in IPS, I have configured the transformation as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.employeeId",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
}

Also in IPS transformation I replaced "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName" with "sourcePath": "$.employeeId".

Users are provisioned with IPS.

In IAS, the "Login Name" field for users receives the employee_id, for example, 1111. These users are then provisioned to SAC and have the employee_id (1111) in the "USER ID" field as well.

When I try to test SSO and select "Choose a user attribute to map to your Identity Provider" -> User attribute set = "USER ID", the user is not found in SAC.

The user accesses the SAC link and enters their email address. The user is authenticated in Azure, but in SAC, the user is not found.

I need help.

I can't solve this by myself.

Thank you very much !!!

entry.title: cloud
SAP Cloud ALM - new user can't logon to activate
entry.content:
What could i be missing? 

  • User is created in IAS and verified to be there.
  • We have SSO using Entra/azure AD.
  • connection between ALM and IAS is as was setup initially during provisioning.
  • Create the user in ALM and assign roles
  • Email sent to user with inivitation URL
  • user clicks on url and then gets standard logon screen (seems to be pointing at correct IAS tenant.
  • user enters email address and enterprise password
  • Logon error.

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone and Employee Central Service Center on different tenants
entry.content:
Hi,

our environment is composed by:

  • Employee Central Service Center (ECSC) on Test connected to IAS test
  • SAP SuccessFactors Development, Work Zone Development connected to IAS Development

BTP Subaccount was Created for Work Zone development. Another BTP subaccount was created for ECSC on test.

Our objective is to connect ECSC to Work Zone dev to use the standard "Ticket Overview" UI card in order to show open ticket for a specific user. 

Any idea on how can we proceed with this complex landscape? We see a lot of issues, in particular the authentication (users for ECSC are connected to IAS test, users in Work Zone are connected to IAS dev) and the deploy of ECSC on two BTP subaccounts at the same moment.

Thank you!
